steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $-6x^{6}+3x^{3}-12x+13$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Group by degree: $x^6$ term: $-6x^6$. $x^3$ term: $3x^3$. $x$ terms: $-4x - 8x = -12x$. Constants: $9 + 4 = 13$. Result: $$-6x^6 + 3x^3 - 12x + 13.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: $-3x^9 - 12x - 13$. Wrong — you cannot combine $3x^3$ and $-6x^6$ into $-3x^9$ (different degrees), and the constant sign is wrong.
  • B: $-6x^6 + 3x^3 - 12x + 13$. Matches the simplified expression. Correct.
  • C: $-6x^6 + 3x^3 + 4x + 5$. Wrong — sign on $-8x$ and on $-(-4)$ were mishandled.
  • D: $-3x^9 - 4x + 5$. Wrong — same illegal exponent addition and arithmetic errors.
That confirms B is correct.
